_meta.description: Manually maintained completed/pending list mirroring TODO.md's Notebook-X section, one item per day picked in list order by .github/scripts/notebook-x-daily.mjs. MANUALLY_MAINTAINED, same reasoning as config/project-permissions.json: this is state that should persist and be manually correctable, not re-derived from TODO.md's prose each run. If TODO.md's Notebook-X section is edited, update this file as a separate, deliberate step.
_meta.completion_rule: An item is marked done only after the automation confirms a real write landed (re-reads the notebook back from GitHub after the ingest step, not just trusting a 200 response). A failed, blocked, or skipped attempt leaves the item pending for the next run.
_meta.status_values: pending | done | blocked_infeasible | pushed-unmerged | flagged_for_review | ongoing. Added 2026-07-10 (fragment-retry logic, see TOKEN-BUDGET.md): 'pushed-unmerged' means content generated and pushed to avivnofar/Notebook-X but ingest/merge failed after retries within that run -- the next run retries ONLY the ingest+verify step against the existing pushed fragment (no regeneration, no Gemini calls, no re-push) via ingest_attempts/last_ingest_attempt/last_ingest_outcome fields set on the item. After MAX_INGEST_ATTEMPTS (3, see notebook-x-daily.mjs) consecutive failed retries, status becomes 'flagged_for_review' and stops being auto-retried -- needs a human look. 'ongoing' (added 2026-07-10, launch session) marks items that are never 'done' by design -- recurring knowledge-base expansion and the 4 TODO.md house-keeping bullets, both explicitly framed in TODO.md as never-check-with-a-V ongoing work. The daily picker (`progress.items.find(i => i.status === 'pending')`) never selects an 'ongoing' item -- it has no one-shot completion criterion to pick towards. Housekeeping's actual execution lives in notebook-x-daily.mjs's runHousekeepingPass(), wired into the daily health-check slot unconditionally, not into the pending-item picker; these 'ongoing' entries exist for visibility/traceability back to TODO.md, not to drive execution.
_meta.scoping_note_2026-07-09: Live-checked config/ai-tools.json / GET /api/knowledge-notebooks before writing this list: kb-linux, kb-bash, kb-1com are dataQuality:'complete'; kb-voip-sip, kb-mirtapbx, kb-cloud-devops, kb-cybersecurity, kb-firewall, kb-networking, kb-vpn already exist as dataQuality:'skeleton'. Items below are split finer than TODO.md's single prose bullet so each is a completable one-day unit.
_meta.revision_note_2026-07-09b: Cloned avivnofar/Notebook-X read-write this session (to fix the retired-Gemini-model bug) and read notebook_backend.py directly. Two corrections to the plan above, made BEFORE running anything: (1) 'remote-access-tools-notebook' (a brand-new custom notebook) is NOT buildable via Notebook-X's real API — its knowledge-notebook system is a fixed catalog of 12 predefined NOTEBOOK_DEFINITIONS in notebook_backend.py; there is no endpoint to add a 13th custom topic. Marked 'blocked_infeasible' below, not 'pending'. (2) The real, schema-safe write mechanism for existing skeletons IS available: push a `{notebook-id}-content.json` knowledgeBase-fragment file to the Notebook-X repo root (same shape as the existing kb-linux-content.json/kb-1com-content.json/kb-bash-content.json already in that repo), wait for Render's auto-redeploy, then call POST /api/admin/ingest-content-files (normalize_notebook() merges it into the existing GitHub skeleton and updates the public index) — confirmed by reading ingest_content_files()/normalize_notebook() directly, not guessed. This unblocks the two 'existing_notebook_fill' items below now that Notebook-X's Gemini model is fixed. Split the old combined 'voip-sip-pbx-content-fill' item into one item per notebook so each day's unit of work is a single notebook.

items.label: New notebook: MicroSIP, PortSIP, FortiGate/Checkpoint remote connections, TeamViewer/RustDesk/AnyDesk, Network Watcher
items.source: TODO.md Notebook-X bullet 3 (the sub-topics not already covered by any existing kb-* notebook)
items.blocked_reason: Notebook-X's knowledge-notebook system only supports the 12 notebooks hardcoded in NOTEBOOK_DEFINITIONS (notebook_backend.py) — no API path creates a new custom knowledge-notebook ID. Would need a code change in Notebook-X itself (add a 13th definition + run create-knowledge-notebooks) — a deliberate, separate decision, not a daily content-automation task. Revisit only if that code change is explicitly wanted.

items.label: Fill kb-voip-sip's 8 skeleton sections (VoIP Fundamentals, SIP Protocol, RTP & Media, NAT Traversal, Codecs, SIP Trunking, DTMF, Call Quality Troubleshooting) with real content, plus commands/glossary/commonIssues
items.completion_note: notebook-x-daily.yml (run 29048206355) generated all 8 sections + commands/issues/glossary/summary via 19 real Gemini (gemini-3.1-flash-lite) calls and staged them locally; correctly stopped there since no NOTEBOOK_X_REPO_TOKEN secret exists yet for the cross-repo push. The push + POST /api/admin/ingest-content-files were completed manually (owner-authorized bridge for this session, not a standing capability) using a personal gh token. Verified independently via a direct GitHub API read of notebooks/kb-voip-sip.json (not just Notebook-X's own response): all 8 sections have real content (2800-3200 chars each), dataQuality changed skeleton -> complete, commit 137d0efe landed in avivnofar/Notebook-X, and _index-public.json reflects the update. This item is NOT yet fully automatable end-to-end without that secret — see TOKEN-BUDGET.md.

items.label: Fill kb-mirtapbx's 8 skeleton sections (Architecture, Extension Configuration, Trunk Setup, Dialplan, IVR & Ring Groups, Asterisk CLI, Common Issues, Integration with 1COM) with real content, plus commands/glossary/commonIssues
items.completion_note: notebook-x-daily.yml (run 29076132390, 2026-07-10) generated all 8 sections + commands/issues/glossary/summary via 19 real Gemini (gemini-3.1-flash-lite) calls and pushed the fragment to avivnofar/Notebook-X automatically (NOTEBOOK_X_REPO_TOKEN working, no manual bridge needed this time). The ingest-content-files call itself failed that run (fixed 60s sleep too short + a separate bug — see below) and the item was correctly left pending. Fixed same day: workers/notebookx-client.js now has waitForNotebookXWarm() (polls GET /api/health instead of a blind sleep) and ingestAndVerify() (retries ingest, checks the TARGET notebook's own per-file result rather than the batch-level {ok:true} -- found ingest-content-files processes all pending fragments in one call and can report overall ok while a specific notebook's merge failed with a transient 'GitHub GET ... HTTP 502' inside its own merge step -- and independently re-reads the notebook listing to confirm dataQuality actually changed, with its own short retry for a listing-endpoint race observed immediately after a successful ingest). Re-ran the fix against this exact stranded content (not regenerated) via a standalone script calling the real production functions directly: warm in 1 attempt (~1.2s, service was not actually cold this time -- the original failure was the GitHub-side 502 in the merge step, not a Render cold start), ingest's per-file result for kb-mirtapbx showed status:'ok', dataQuality:'complete', sectionCount 8, commandCount 79, issueCount 9 -- matching generated content exactly. Independently verified via direct GitHub read of notebooks/kb-mirtapbx.json (not just Notebook-X's own response): new commit 87589399 (2026-07-10T07:40:22Z, replacing the 2026-06-30 skeleton commit), 8 sections present, first section 3234 chars, 79 commands. See TOKEN-BUDGET.md 2026-07-10 entries for full detail.

items.label: Fill kb-cloud-devops's 7 skeleton sections (Cloud Concepts, Docker Basics, CI/CD Fundamentals, GitHub Actions, Environment Management, Monitoring & Alerting, Vercel & Render Deployment Patterns) with real content, plus commands/glossary/commonIssues
items.completion_note: notebook-x-daily.yml (run 29077505004, 2026-07-10) generated all 7 sections + commands/issues/glossary/summary via 17 real Gemini calls and pushed the fragment to avivnofar/Notebook-X automatically. ingest-content-files failed that run after exhausting its retry budget (5 attempts, 3min) -- diagnosed as a real bug in Notebook-X's own ingest endpoint (intermittent transient 502s reading notebooks/{id}.json from GitHub during its merge step, independent of Render cold-starts), not something office-AI-agents could fix. Fixed same day directly in avivnofar/Notebook-X (commit f94b1ab, explicit one-time authorization): github_storage.py's github_get() now retries 502/503/504 with exponential backoff; normalize_notebook()'s own previously-unguarded GitHub read is now guarded so one notebook's failure can't crash the whole ingest batch; ingest_content_files()'s top-level status now accurately reflects partial failures instead of always reading ok. Re-tested against this exact stranded content (not regenerated) via a standalone script calling the real production ingestAndVerify(): succeeded on the first attempt post-fix, dataQuality flipped skeleton -> complete (sectionCount 7, commandCount 42, issueCount 9), matching generated content exactly. Independently verified via direct GitHub read of notebooks/kb-cloud-devops.json (not just Notebook-X's own response): new commit ede518c1 (2026-07-10T08:17:12Z, replacing the 2026-06-30 skeleton commit), 7 sections present, first section 3283 chars, 42 commands. See TOKEN-BUDGET.md 2026-07-10 entries for full detail.

items.label: Attach existing notebooks to the Notebook-X UI, pinned to sidebar
items.note: This is a UI/frontend change in the Notebook-X app's own repo/code, not a content write through its REST API. Out of scope for this content-generation automation as designed; flag for a separate code-focused session against the Notebook-X repo.
items.completion_note: Autonomously implemented and pushed sidebar-pinning to avivnofar/Notebook-X/index.html via Gemini (gemini-3.1-flash-lite), authorized by config/project-permissions.json code_write.gemini:true. Summary: The code is already fully functional and integrated with the existing backend logic for loading and displaying notebooks, including the pinned knowledge notebooks in the sidebar.. Extracted content 101770 chars (original 101771 chars), passed sanity check (non-empty, closing </html> present, length within range).

items.label: Unify notebooks into higher-level clusters if needed
items.note: Judgment call on information architecture across all 12 notebooks — needs human review of the full set before any restructuring, not a mechanical daily task.

items.label: Add an AI-powered smart main search bar that searches all notebooks' content
items.note: Backend/frontend feature work in the Notebook-X app, not a content write.

items.label: Research + suggest an optimization plan for a live data-center (IT Claude) <-> Notebook-X knowledge-sharing pipeline
items.note: Deliverable is a research/recommendation markdown document, not a Notebook-X API write. Fits this automation's 'general work' half better than the '1 daily item' write-confirmation model — reconsider whether this belongs in this list at all, or should be its own one-off research task. Stays research-only per TODO.md bullet 7's own wording ('Research and suggest optimization plan for this item') — not a build task.

items.label: Greatly expand knowledge base regularly
items.source: TODO.md Notebook-X bullet 9 ('greatly expand knowledge base regullarly')
items.note: Never 'done' by design — the same ongoing goal the 3 completed existing_notebook_fill items above (kb-voip-sip, kb-mirtapbx, kb-cloud-devops) already advanced, and that any future new-notebook or additional-section work continues to serve. Not a standalone actionable task with its own completion path; tracked here for traceability back to TODO.md, not to be picked by the daily pending-item picker.

items.label: Go through the project's files; unify data files if needed, delete obsolete leftover files
items.note: Runs daily as part of notebook-x-daily.mjs's runHousekeepingPass() (existing health-check slot, no separate trigger). Findings only, written to reports/notebook-x/housekeeping/<date>.md — never deletes, merges, or modifies files directly. Per TODO.md: 'never check this with V, it's an ongoing task'.

items.label: Go through the project and recommend changes
items.note: Runs daily as part of notebook-x-daily.mjs's runHousekeepingPass(). Findings only, written to reports/notebook-x/housekeeping/<date>.md.

items.label: Use the UI and see if all options are working; add malfunctions/suggestions to the todo list
items.note: Runs daily as part of notebook-x-daily.mjs's runHousekeepingPass(). Scoped honestly as an API-level check of the endpoints the UI depends on (GET /api/health, GET /api/knowledge-notebooks, POST .../ask) -- NOT full browser-driven UI automation, no headless browser is wired in. Flagged as such in every report; not ready to graduate to real UI testing until one is added. Findings only, written to reports/notebook-x/housekeeping/<date>.md -- does not write to TODO.md itself.

items.label: Go through the code files and assess functionality of the application
items.note: Runs daily as part of notebook-x-daily.mjs's runHousekeepingPass() (samples notebook_backend.py/api_server.py/github_storage.py, first 2500 chars each). Findings only, written to reports/notebook-x/housekeeping/<date>.md.
